data.title: Vale Indonesia 2024 nickel ore output at 15.2 million metric tons, company data
data.description:
Nickel miner Vale Indonesia Tbk PT presented to a parliamentary hearing on Thursday:

  • 2024 NICKEL ORE OUTPUT AT 15.2 MILLION METRIC TONS VS 13.52 MLN METRIC TONS IN 2023

  • 2024 NICKEL MATTE OUTPUT AT 71,311 METRIC TONS VS 70,728 METRIC TONS IN 2023

  • 2024 NICKEL MATTE SALES AT 72,625 METRIC TONS VS 70,190 METRIC TONS IN 2023

data.title: Hsin Ba Ba Taps Barson Construction for NT$319 Million Residential Project
data.description:
Hsin Ba Ba appointed Barson Construction to build a residential project on its land for NT$319.2 million, according to a Wednesday Taiwan Exchange filing.

The project in New Taipei City, Taiwan, is expected to be completed within 1,200 working days.

Shares of the aluminum can manufacturer gained marginally recently.

data.title: Home Product Center Target Price Lowered to THB10.20 From THB13.00 by Maybank Securities (Thailand) >HMPRO.TH
Asian stocks rebound as US inflation cools; trade war woes persist
data.description:
Key points:
  • EM Asia stocks rebound from previous session's losses
  • Malaysian stocks rise as much as 1.5%
  • Likely reciprocal tariff woes from April in EM Asia - analyst

Most emerging Asian equities rebounded on Thursday, following the release of cooler U.S. inflation data for February, although the threat of an escalating global trade war continues to loom in the background.

The MSCI's gauge for emerging Asian equities (.MIMS00000PUS) rose as much as 0.5% in the day, rebounding from a 0.4% drop at close on Wednesday.

Data from the U.S. showed that consumer prices increased less than expected in February, but investors fret that the improvement is likely temporary against the backdrop of aggressive tariffs on imports that are expected to raise the costs of most goods in the months ahead.

However, the positive sentiment from the cooling U.S. inflation print supported a rebound in both emerging Asia stocks and currencies, according to Poon Panichpibool, market strategist at Krung Thai Bank.

Stocks in Kuala Lumpur rose the most, advancing as much as 1.5% and snapping a five-session losing streak, while Thai equities climbed nearly 1%.

However, as April approaches, concerns over reciprocal tariffs have surfaced, with U.S. President Donald Trump continuing to impose tariffs on neighbouring countries. This could potentially have a negative spell on assets in emerging Asia, Poon warned.

"Countries such as India, Thailand, Philippines ... collect higher tariffs from the U.S. when we compare to what the U.S. collects from them ... we could definitely face reciprocal tariffs for sure, which could be quite negative for EM Asia," he said.

Stocks in Indonesia were an outlier on Thursday, falling as much as 0.7% in early trade.

A few large banks, including Bank Mandiri (Persero) and Bank Rakyat Indonesia (Persero) , pulled the benchmark lower in early trade.

A nearly 30% drop in Indonesian government revenues in January, which comes as President Prabowo Subianto implements big spending plans, has raised market concerns about fiscal sustainability and a potential jump in borrowing.

Currencies in emerging Asia strengthened, with the Indian rupee , the Indonesian rupiah and the Philippine peso rising around 0.2% each.

A rise in global trade tensions and worries over U.S. recession risks have rattled global markets and sparked huge volatility in the foreign exchange market, as traders seesaw between relief and angst over Trump's whipsawing policy changes.

HIGHLIGHTS:

** POLL-BOJ set to hold rates this month, hike to 0.75% in Q3, most likely July

** Duterte takes responsibility for Philippines drug war, anticipates long ICC battle

data.title: Swire Pacific Reports Profit Decline
data.description:
(04:20 GMT) *Swire Pacific 2024 Net HK$4.32B Vs. Net HK$28.85B >0019.HK
(04:20 GMT) *Swire Pacific 2024 Rev HK$81.97B Vs. HK$94.82B >0019.HK
(04:20 GMT) *Swire Pacific: Changes in Fair Value of Investment Properties Weighed on Results >0019.HK
(04:21 GMT) *Swire Pacific: Beverages Division Profit Fell Due to Disposal of Swire Coca-Cola USA in 2H 2023 >0019.HK
(04:24 GMT) *Swire Pacific Declares Second Interim Dividend of HK$2.10 Per A Share, HK$0.42 Per B Share >0019.HK
(04:54 GMT) Swire Pacific Reports Profit Decline

By Amanda Lee

Swire Pacific reported sharply lower profit for 2024, citing changes in fair value of its investment properties and a profit decline at its beverages division.

The Hong Kong-based conglomerate's net profit slid 85% to 4.32 billion Hong Kong dollars, equivalent to US$556 million, compared with the prior year, the company said Thursday.

Revenue fell 13.5% to HK$81.97 billion.

Swire Pacific said the decline in underlying profit attributable to shareholders was driven by changes in the fair value of investment properties, which fell 71% to HK$10.47 billion in 2024.

Its Swire Coca-Cola business reported recurring profit of HK$1.39 billion last year, compared with HK$2.39 billion in 2023. The decline in profit was largely due to the disposal of Swire Coca-Cola USA in the second half of 2023, the company said.

The company declared a second interim dividend of HK$2.10 per A share and HK$0.42 per B share, taking the full-year dividend to HK$3.35 per A share and HK$0.67 per B share. That's a 5% increase on the full-year ordinary dividends for 2023, Swire Pacific said.

Write to Amanda Lee at amanda.lee@wsj.com

data.title: Del Monte Pacific's Attributable Loss Widens in Fiscal Q3; Shares Down 3%
data.description:
Del Monte Pacific's attributable loss to owners widened to $35.9 million in the fiscal third quarter ended Jan. 31, from $29.0 million a year earlier, according to a filing with the Singapore Exchange on Wednesday.

Loss per share widened to $0.0185 compared with $0.0149 in the comparable period.

Turnover rose 2.5% year over year to $663.0 million from $646.7 million, mainly due to higher exports of fresh and packaged pineapple products.

Shares of the company were down nearly 3% in recent trading.

data.title: Telefónica to Sell Majority Stake in Colombian Business to Millicom
data.description:
Telefónica agreed to sell its controlling 67.5% stake in its Colombian business to Millicom International Cellular's Millicom Spain, according to a Wednesday release.

Under the deal, the Spanish telecommunications company will sell its interest in Telefónica Colombia, d/b/a Colombia Telecomunicaciones, through the takeover proposal that Millicom lodged for the business. As part of the offer, the Luxembourg-based fixed and mobile telecommunications company will also purchase the remaining 32.5% stake in ColTel from La Nación and other investors.

The agreed purchase price was $400 million but was adjusted to $362 million after net debt evolution, working capital, and foreign exchange rates as of Sept. 30, 2024.

Completion of the transaction is subject to customary closing conditions, including the approval of relevant regulators.

data.title: TSH Resources Posts Production Report for February
data.description:
TSH Resources a Malaysia-based company involved in oil palm cultivation and wood products, published its production results for February, according to a Wednesday bourse filing.

During the month, the company produced 66,022 metric tons of fresh fruit bunches, the primary raw material for palm oil production.

TSH Resources also produced 17,158 metric tons of crude palm oil, 3,440 metric tons of palm kernel, and 65,547 kilograms of rubber during the month.

The dual-listed company's log production volume stood at 1,286 cubic meters in February, the filing said.

data.title: Japanese investors pull out of foreign bonds after European debt selloff
data.description:
Japanese investors sold the highest quantum of foreign bonds in over a month in the week through March 8, turning cautious due to a selloff in Eurozone debt markets triggered by Germany's plans for robust fiscal spending.

They sold a net 355.9 billion yen ($2.40 billion) worth of long-term foreign bonds during the week, their largest weekly net sales since February 1, data from Japan's Ministry of Finance showed.

Germany's 10-year bond yield reached a 16-1/2-month peak of 2.938% on Wednesday as Friedrich Merz, likely to be the next chancellor, sought support for significantly increased state borrowing to stimulate the economy and enhance military spending.

Japanese investors have allocated approximately 2.96 trillion yen to long-term foreign debt securities this year, a decrease from net purchases of about 5.8 trillion yen during the same period last year.

Meanwhile, Japanese market participants took advantage of a stronger yen to boost their position in foreign shares, which are available at reduced prices following a recent global selloff in equities.

They pumped a robust 1.26 trillion yen into foreign shares, the biggest amount for a week since August 3.

Meanwhile, foreign investors bought a net 686.4 billion yen worth of long-term foreign bonds, extending net purchases into a fourth consecutive week. They also snapped up a net 1.1 trillion yen worth of short-term instruments, the highest in seven weeks.

Simultaneously, Japanese stocks faced a net 220.5 billion yen worth of foreign outflows, with cross-border investors withdrawing funds for a sixth straight week.

($1 = 148.1000 yen)

data.title: Japan's largest industrial union says its members received average 5.37% increase in wages
data.description:
UA Zensen is Japan's largest industrial trade union and represents workers mostly at small and medium-sized firms. They had previously requested for a standard 6% increase in total wages. As for full-time workers, the desired target was a 6.11% increase. So, the figure here is not the best although the over 6% jump was rather ambitious to begin with. But we still have to wait on the wages for part-time workers - which is typically higher.

For some context, the union is seeking a 7.16% increase in wages for part-time workers.

data.title: Resolution Minerals to Raise AU$1.7 Million to Advance Antimony Projects; Shares Fall 14%
data.description:
Resolution Minerals received commitments to raise a total of AU$1.7 million to advance its recently acquired Antimony projects, according to a Thursday Australian bourse filing.

A total of around 154.5 million shares will be issued at AU$0.011 per share under the placement, as approved by its shareholders.

The company will receive AU$150,000 of the amount at the end of the month.

Participants will also be issued one option for every two shares issued under the placement, for no additional consideration. The options will have an exercise price of AU$0.018 per share and will expire on July 31, 2028.

Resolution Minerals' shares fell 14% in recent trading on Thursday.

data.title: Germany’s fiscal pivot gives Europe rare hope: podcast
data.description:
Follow on Apple or Spotify. Listen on the Reuters app. Read the episode transcript.

Listen to the podcast

The nation known for anti-deficit orthodoxy faces an economic slowdown and US antagonism. Likely Chancellor Friedrich Merz’s response: boost spending by more than 500 bln euros. In this week’s Viewsroom podcast Breakingviews columnists discuss the jolt to the whole continent.

Follow @JMAGuilford and @aimeedonnellan on X

FURTHER READING

‘Merz spurt’ will see Europe CEOs rethink US pivot

data.title: Market Chatter: Morgan Stanley Cuts Australian Shares Rating, The Australian Says
data.description:
Morgan Stanley cut its rating for Australian equities as it expects Australian companies to underperform compared to global markets because of higher valuations and trade exposure to the US, The Australian reported on Thursday, citing Morgan Stanley strategists led by Daniel Blake.

A tighter fiscal policy combined with increased tariffs is expected to impact growth, the report said.

Apart from Australia, the investment bank kept its underweight rating on South Korea and New Zealand, per the report.

(Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.)

data.title: Market Chatter: Naver Launches 3D Motion Capture Studio for Chzzk Streamers
data.description:
Naver launched three-dimensional content production studio Motion Stage, enabling streamers on its platform Chzzk to create high-quality 3D videos, Pulse News reported Wednesday.

The studio uses advanced motion capture technology, allowing users to translate full-body movements into lifelike animations for sci-fi scenes or virtual idols. Streamers can now use the facility, with professional staff available to assist in production, the report said.

The move aligns with the increasing demand for the virtual streaming market, which is projected to grow to $4.5 billion by 2030 from $2.86 billion in 2025, the report said, citing Mordor Intelligence.

(Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.)

data.title: Al Ramz Lands Liquidity Provision Deal from Adnoc Gas
data.description:
Al Ramz Corp. Investment and Development's (DFM:ALRAMZ) unit Al Ramz Capital secured a deal to deliver liquidity provision services to natural gas distributor Adnoc Gas (ADX:ADNOCGAS).

The financial services provider will begin independent trading of Adnoc Gas' shares from Wednesday following regulatory approvals, according to a same-day release.

data.title: Factors to watch on March 13
data.description:
Britain's FTSE 100 CURRENCYCOM:UK100 index is seen opening lower on Thursday, with futures down 0.12%.

* RIO TINTO: JPMorgan Chase holds a 5.36% stake in Australia's mining conglomerate Rio Tinto , , an exchange filing showed.

* HOUSING: Britain's housing market had its slowest month in more than a year in February as a rush by buyers to close deals ahead of the expiry of a tax break slowed, a survey showed.

* LABOUR: British social care providers will have to prioritise hiring foreign care workers already in the country before recruiting from overseas, the government said.

* COST OF LIVING: Britain's poorest households are worse off than those in Slovenia and Malta after nearly two decades of stagnation in UK living standards, The National Institute of Economic and Social Research said.

* BEAZLEY: Beazley has recruited longtime Marsh property executive Lindsay Shipper as its new North America head of commercial property, three property market sources familiar with the matter told Reuters.

* OIL: Oil prices slipped on Thursday after a surge in the previous session on a larger-than-expected draw in U.S. gasoline stocks.

* GOLD : Gold prices edged higher.

* METALS: London metals were mostly down on the backdrop of aggressive Trump tariffs.

* EX-DIVS: Rank Group PLC and Treatt PLC will trade without entitlement to its latest dividend pay-out on Thursday.

* The UK blue-chip index CURRENCYCOM:UK100 closed 0.5% higher on Wednesday, snapping six sessions of declines, its longest losing streak since September.

data.title: China's State-Owned Enterprises Investment in Xinjiang Up 64% in 2024
data.description:
China's central state-owned enterprises (SOE) invested more than 280 billion yuan in Xinjiang in 2024, a 63.7% annual increase, supporting 336 projects in the Uygur autonomous region, Xinhua News reported Wednesday.

The investments cover sectors such as petroleum, natural gas, coal, new energy and transportation.

Chen Weijun, vice chairman of the regional government, said more than 40 central SOEs will invest more than 380 billion yuan in Xinjiang in 2025.

data.title: Dolfines Lands 5-year Rig Inspection Contract with Adnoc
data.description:
Dolfines signed a five-year rig inspection frame contract with the offshore division of Abu Dhabi National Oil Co., d/b/a Adnoc.

The deal involves the inspection of the Jack Up, Barges and Island rigs, with potential annual revenue of over $1 million, the French engineering company said Wednesday. The contract will take effect on April 29.

Additionally, Adnoc's Ghasha concession division extended Dolfines' existing rig inspection contract by two years, starting in March.

data.title: RICS: UK House Price Balance Falls in February
data.description:
The UK Royal Institution of Chartered Surveyors house price balance decreased to 11% in February, from the revised 21% in the previous month, according to data released Thursday.

Analysts expected a 20% reading for the month.

Most respondents see house prices rising over the next twelve months amid expectations of reduced momentum in the short term.

data.title: Mapletree Logistics Trust to Divest Warehouse for SG$8 Million
data.description:
Mapletree Logistics Trust agreed to dispose of its 31 Penjuru Lane property in Singapore for SG$7.8 million, according to a filing with the Singapore Exchange on Wednesday.

JTC Corporation has been granted in-principle approval for the transaction.

The divestment is part of the manager's efforts to rejuvenate the portfolio through divestment of assets that are no longer part of the group's long-term strategy.

The property comprises a three-story warehouse and an ancillary office. The property's remaining land lease is of around seven years.

Following the divestment, the trust's portfolio will consist of 176 properties.

Shares of the trust were up nearly 2% in recent trading.

data.title: Aether Catalyst Solutions, Inc. Announces a Non-Brokered Private Placement
data.description:
Burnaby, British Columbia--(Newsfile Corp. - March 13, 2025) - Aether Catalyst Solutions, Inc. (FSE: 2QZ) ("ATHR" or the "Company") announces that it proposes to raise up to $75,000 through a non-brokered private placement of up to 1,875,000 units at a price of $0.04 per unit. Each unit will consist of one common share and one-half of a transferable common share purchase warrant of the Company, with each whole warrant exercisable for a period of 12 months from the date of closing at a price of $0.10 per share.

Finder's fees or commissions may be payable to certain eligible persons. All securities issued in connection with the private placement are subject to a 4-month hold period in Canada.

The terms of the financing are subject to applicable securities laws and regulatory approval. Additional details regarding the Offering can be found in the Form 9 filed in accordance with the rules of the Canadian Securities Exchange, a copy of which is accessible at www.thecse.com under the Company's profile.

The gross proceeds of the private placement will be used for general working capital purposes.

The Company confirms that there is no material fact or material change about the Company that has not already been generally disclosed. Closing is expected to occur March 19, 2025.

ABOUT THE COMPANY:

Aether Catalyst Solutions, Inc. is focused on providing an order of magnitude cost reduction in automotive catalytic converter catalyst, while meeting, or exceeding government emission standards. Aether is working to quickly advance its technology through rapid screening of new materials directed at enhancing end of life conversion levels after accelerated aging. While Aether's primary focus has been automotive applications, the company is also developing catalysts to address small motors emissions - a significant contributor to urban air pollution.

FOR FURTHER INFORMATION PLEASE CONTACT:

Aether Catalyst Solutions, Inc.

Paul Woodward

President

Tel: 604 690-3797

http://www.aethercatalyst.com

The Canadian Securities Exchange ("CSE") or any other securities regulatory authority has not reviewed and does not accept responsibility for the adequacy or accuracy of this management prepared news release.

NOT FOR DISTRIBUTION TO U.S. NEWSWIRE SERVICES OR FOR

DISSEMINATION IN THE UNITED STATES

To view the source version of this press release, please visit https://www.newsfilecorp.com/release/244428

data.title: Jet2 Sets Final Repurchase Price for GBP302 Million Bond Buyback
data.description:
Jet2 on Wednesday announced the final repurchase price for 387.4 million pounds sterling of 1.625% guaranteed senior unsecured convertible bonds due 2026.

The leisure travel company will pay 103,577.29 pounds per 100,000 in principal amount of bonds accepted for purchase, which amounted to 301.5 million pounds.

Settlement is expected on March 18.

data.title: Global Forex and Fixed Income Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering FX and Fixed Income. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires throughout the day.

The cooler February U.S. inflation print provides some relief, but the test of tariffs' impact on prices lies in the months ahead, UOB says. U.S. tariff uncertainty and other policies keep risk for inflation elevated, says senior economist Alvin Liew in a note. While goods inflation appeared tame in February, it is expected to push higher as President Trump's tariffs on China and other major trading partners as well as on key products start to hit home, he says. While the downside risk to growth has increased, Trump policy uncertainty would continue to argue the case for the Fed to stay on hold for longer, Liew says. UOB expects the Fed to deliver a 25bp cut in 2Q and then hold rates for the rest of the year. (monica.gupta@wsj.com)

data.title: Cooler U.S. Inflation Offers Reprieve, but Fed May Cut Just Once This Year — Market Talk
data.description: The cooler February U.S. inflation print provides some relief, but the test of tariffs' impact on prices lies in the months ahead, UOB says. data.title: China, Hong Kong shares weighed down by tech stocks
data.description:
China and Hong Kong shares fell on Thursday, pressured by tech and AI-related firms, although gains in banking and energy stocks helped cushion the broader decline.

** At the midday break, the Shanghai Composite index was down 0.4% and China's blue-chip CSI300 index fell 0.2%.

** The Hang Seng Index weakened 0.7% in Hong Kong, while the Hang Seng Tech Index slipped 2.2%.

** Tech shares led the losses in both onshore and offshore trading. The CSI AI Index dropped 2.2%, heading for its biggest single-day decline in two weeks, while Alibaba and Tencent (700.HK) retreated 2.7% and 1.4%, respectively, in Hong Kong.

** However, the banking index (.CSI399986) climbed 0.6% and energy shares rallied 3.2%, helping limit declines.

** On Wednesday, Donald Trump threatened to escalate a global trade war with further tariffs on European Union goods, as major U.S. trading partners said they would retaliate for trade barriers already erected by the U.S. president.

** The resilience of Chinese stocks in the face of the U.S. equity selloff could be unsustainable as the economies could deteriorate as U.S. demand – the sole pillar of global growth in the past two years – vanishes and tariffs bite, analysts at BCA Research said in a note.

** Around the region, MSCI's Asia ex-Japan stock index (.MIAPJ0000PUS) fell 0.49% while Japan's Nikkei index was up 0.42%.

data.title: Nebraska governor signs bill to regulate crypto ATMs, citing growing fraud
data.description:
The governor of Nebraska, Jim Pillen, has signed legislation to protect against cryptocurrency fraud as crypto ATM crime skyrockets in the United States.

“Cryptocurrency is an important, emerging industry, and we’ve been working hard to build Nebraska into a cryptocurrency leader,” said Governor Pillen on March 12 following the signing of the bill. 

“An important part of these efforts is to make sure that we have guardrails to prevent criminals from taking advantage of Nebraskans,” he added.

The bipartisan legislation establishes the “Controllable Electronic Record Fraud Prevention Act,” which is designed to help combat fraud and protect users of crypto kiosks and ATMs.

According to the Federal Trade Commission, victims have lost over $65 million to crypto ATM fraud in the first half of 2024. “Fraud losses at BTMs (Bitcoin ATMs) are skyrocketing, increasing nearly tenfold from 2020 to 2023,” the commission reported in September. 

The bill, LB 609, was introduced on Jan. 22 by Senator Eliot Bostar. It stipulates that crypto ATM and kiosk operators must be licensed under Nebraska’s Money Transmitters Act and registered and approved by the Department of Banking and Finance. Operators must provide quarterly reports on kiosk locations, names and transaction data.

It also implements transaction limits of $2,000 per day for new users and $5,000 per day for existing customers, and fees cannot exceed 18% of the transaction value. 

New customers who report fraud within 90 days can receive a full refund including fees, while existing customers can be refunded for the fees associated with fraudulent transactions.

Kiosk operators must also display fraud warnings and appoint a compliance officer to enforce fraud prevention measures, it states. 

The US crypto ATM network shrunk by more than 1,200 machines earlier this month after Illinois Senator Dick Durbin introduced similar legislation. 

“Nebraska is open for business in the cryptocurrency space,” commented state Department of Banking director Kelly Lammers, who added, “those that target our citizens … using crypto ATMs as part of their transfer method, we will soon have a team that will be watching even more closely.”

While being supportive of crypto, Nebraska has yet to join the 21 US states that have proposed legislation to establish strategic crypto reserves, according to the Bitcoin Reserve Monitor.

data.title: Anglo American Unit Prices Redemption of Notes
data.description:
Anglo American (, AGL.JO) subsidiary Anglo American Capital priced its offer to repurchase multiple series of US dollar- and euro-denominated notes.

Based on the results of the offer launched on Feb. 26, the mining company's unit will redeem $394 million of 4% senior notes due September 2027, $110.7 million of 4.750% senior notes due April 2027, $379.9 million of 2.250% senior notes due March 2028, and 126 million euros of 4.5% guaranteed notes due September 2028 at a per-note price of $987.25, $1,002.96, $936.36 and 1,052.24 euros, respectively.

Anglo American Capital indicated that it has sole discretion to increase the maximum acceptance amount for the redemption of the notes. Early settlement is expected by Friday.

data.title: Zydus Lifesciences' Venture Capital Arm Investing in Illexcor Therapeutics
data.description:
Zynext Ventures USA, the venture capital arm of Zydus Lifesciences , has agreed to invest of an undisclosed amount in biopharmaceutical company Illexcor Therapeutics.

The investment is aimed at advancing novel oral therapy for sickle cell disease, according to a Wednesday filing to the Indian stock exchanges.

Illexcor is developing oral therapies for sickle cell disease. The lead asset, currently in preclinical development, is designed to bind to Hemoglobin S and effectively block polymerization and sickling, offering the potential for disease-modifying clinical benefits.

Zydus Lifesciences shares were up nearly 1% in recent trade.
